reasoning: The latest data point available as of November 9, 2025, for the 10-year real interest rate estimate from the Cleveland Fed is 1.608. Although yields generally rose in October (pushing this estimate up), news from early November indicates that 10-year Treasury yields have started to move lower. A linear trend model suggests downward momentum with an expected value below the current 1.608 mark, assigning roughly a 30% chance of an increase by early December. However, this economic series typically behaves like a random walk, making short-term trend predictions unreliable and heavily pulling the base rate expectation back toward 50%. Adjusting for the recent drop in yields and the downward momentum, I assign a 42% probability of an increase by December 2025. For the February 2026 date, increased forecasting horizon dictates a reversion closer to the random walk baseline of 48%.
